reason: Modifying /etc/fstab to add the noexec option changes filesystem mount behavior. While the change is generally recommended, it can disrupt services or applications that rely on executing binaries from /dev/shm and may cause boot issues if the fstab entry is malformed. Human verification is needed before applying.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; potential service disruption; boot failure if fstab syntax is incorrect; applications expecting exec permissions in /dev/shm
